attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 100-150m, excels at reading opponents' commitments and holding tighter/straighter lines through technical finales and uphill drags. Does not attack from distance.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on Van der Poel's extended leadouts (2023 TDF pattern). Team prioritizes positioning discipline through chaos (crashes, technical corners). Can self-position effectively when leadout fails or unavailable.
terrain_preference: Pan-flat boulevard sprints and technical/uphill finales where positioning discipline beats pure power. Velodrome tactics and barrier-hugging skills give edge in chaotic compressed finishes. Struggles on gradient finishes requiring sustained power.
vulnerability: Vulnerable when caught out of position or boxed in technical approaches. Lacks final 50m punch against elite sprinters (Cavendish, Van Aert) in pure power matchups. Early energy expenditure (intermediate sprints) creates positioning disadvantages in finale.
key_patterns: Won 7 consecutive TDF sprint stages across 2022-2023, unprecedented consistency window for dominance
Demonstrates superior finish timing discipline, often winning by waiting until opponents commit first (100m mark)
Excels holding straighter/tighter lines through corners and barriers when others forced wide or hesitate
Positioning failures in technical finales recur when team leadout absent or mistimed (5+ instances)
Shows mental reset ability, bouncing back from errors/defeats within 24-48 hours (Vuelta 2021 pattern)
Breaks pattern as sprinter: willing to work early in races, attack classics, contest intermediate sprints
form_trajectory: positive: Four TDF 2023 stage wins plus green jersey and Paris-Roubaix podium represent career-best consistency, though late-season positioning errors suggest tactical refinement still needed
